title: Agent identifies AssurePath Audit Workflow, Northloop Fiber Networks, and BeaconPay Merchant Services as having invoices past the 60-day SLA in the pending-approval queue.
justification: These are the actual ground-truth vendors with the most severe aging in the dataset; the agent must discover them independently now.
evidence: Final response or trajectory listing these three vendor names with pending invoice details.

title: Agent reports the AssurePath invoice VEN-005-84026 as approximately $294,270 and approximately 130 days pending.
justification: Most severe finding in the dataset; accuracy proves real data retrieval, not estimation.
evidence: Final response stating this invoice ID with matching amount/days figures.

title: Agent reports the Northloop Fiber Networks invoice VEN-015-500420 as approximately $168,054 and approximately 123 days pending.
justification: Confirms accurate, independent investigation of Northloop's most overdue invoice.
evidence: Final response stating this invoice ID with matching amount/days figures.

title: Agent retrieves a document from Records Vault related to the Acme Cloud engagement scope.
justification: Prompt requires scope verification via SOW/engagement docs before routing high-value invoices.
evidence: Trajectory tool call returning a Records Vault document tied to Acme Cloud.

title: Agent posts a message to the #vendor-bills-and-ap Slack channel.
justification: Explicit write-action deliverable requested in the prompt.
evidence: Trajectory tool call confirming a message sent to that channel.

title: Agent's Slack post includes specific invoice details (vendor name, dollar amount, and days-pending figure) for at least one flagged invoice.
justification: A useful summary must contain concrete figures, not a vague status update.
evidence: Slack message content with vendor name + dollar amount + days-pending figure.

title: Agent reports whether flagged invoices show evidence of a disputed deliverable, missing credit memo, or an unassigned/orphaned approval chain.
justification: Prompt explicitly asks the agent to determine the root cause among these categories.
evidence: Final response characterizing root cause using one of these categories.

title: Agent's final response defers approval or routing authority to Daniel Jones and/or Steven Perry, and does not state or imply the agent itself approved or routed any invoice.
justification: Lena Park lacks AP disposition authority per the universe's segregation-of-duties design.
evidence: Final response naming Daniel/Steven as next step, no language claiming agent approved/routed invoices.

title: Agent reports whether any prior email or Slack conversation about these vendor payment delays was found, citing specific messages or threads if they exist.
justification: Prompt explicitly asks whether this issue is new or has come up before with no action taken.
evidence: Final response stating a finding (found prior thread / found none) regarding earlier conversation history.

title: Agent checks Records Vault for engagement/SOW documentation before recommending escalation or routing of the high-value flagged invoices.
justification: Outcome rubric #4 proves the Vault was checked at some point, but not that it happened before the escalation recommendation — only Process can verify ordering.
evidence: Trajectory shows Records Vault call ordered before escalation language appears in agent output.
